features.evidence: okay it s been quite a while since i spoke about my 14in m3 max macbook pro which cost me an awful lot of money so it s time for an update and it s been both good and not so good i wasn t convinced i was going to make another video about the macbook pro and that s not because i don t use it anymore because i do it s used every single day in this business it s an absolutely essential tool the reason i wasn t going to revisit this on the channel to cut to the chase is because for some reason the interest levels in macbook pros dies off quite quickly compared to let s say the mac mini or the the macbook air which both of those machines seem to live long in the youtube interest graphs people love any content about those two devices computers whatever they are this not so much i don t really understand why but equally i know there s still a lot of people out there who are thinking about buying one of these and because they are so astronomically expensive i i completely understand if you re taking your time with this decision and if you want to hear from someone who s been using one of these for seven months that s what i ve been doing and i ve got lots of thoughts but before we get into that just a very quick word from today s sponsor which is robo form and robo form is a password manager if you are still storing your passwords in a spreadsheet or even worse on post it notes and stuff you need a password manager so you can put your login details in there your secret notes anything digital that you want to hide away from prying eyes go into robo form and if you work across platform so if you ve got a mac a windows device an android phone an ios device an ipad etc it works across all of them and yes robo form has a no breach history which is very very important and robo form combines ease of use with incredible value for money for instance it has a oneclick login feature which does exactly what it says on the tin you just choose your favorite website or your favorite web app and bos you re in you can also share passwords with friends and family very securely and there s an emergency access feature which means if you for whatever reason can t get to your passwords you have someone designated who can do it for you they ve even got full 24 7 support if anything goes wrong which in my experience with robo form is very very unlikely the best news is i ve got an incredible offer for you with robo for it s an absolute no brainer go and check it out so for those who haven t been following my macbook pro journey i should confirm that about 7 months ago i went from the 16 in version the m1 max 16 in macbook pro to this m3 max 14in version and the reason i did that is because i m traveling more i m on planes trains automobiles and walking around a lot in different countries and things and i got no end of stick for this but that 16 in macbook pro became a massive burden the footprint was just too big big i don t fly first class or business class so i have to make do with those normal trays that most of us get on planes and it didn t fit on them the same thing with trains which is a shame because it s an amazing laptop and i do miss certain elements of it which i ll come on to in a moment this is my well this is the replacement and if you re wondering it s the 14 in macbook pro with the m3 max chip 16 core cpu 40 core gpu 64 gb of unified memory and a 2 tb ssd it cost me i had to myself of this 4,399 was it the right thing to do yes and no i ll come to the no bit in a moment but in terms of the yes i was right that whole thing about the 16 in macbook pro weighing me down it was definitely a thing i do go to the gym etc etc it doesn t really weigh as much as a small country which is something i said in a previous video when you ve got this in your backpack it feels so much lighter and it sits on those seat trays and pretty much any coffee shop table i ve been to in the last 6 months it hasn t been a burden at all and also in my opinion i think the 14 in macbook pro looks better i think it s just i think it suits this size the whole the new macbook pro design is better in 14in argue with me in the comments if you don t agree it s got the sling ability factor which i ve talked about before which is that thing it just feels very satisfying to sling under your arm whereas the 16 in macbook pro is slinging a small country under your arm but there are two things that i miss from the 16 in macbook pro the first thing is that display and that s for one very specific reason i m a video editor so i edit these videos and if i m doing it in here in this studio that s fine i ve got two great big benq monitors if i m editing at home in the kitchen this is just a bit too small which is why i use this with this and this is oh it s been a while hasn t since this has been on the channel any excuse just check the front display isn t cracked vision pro so since i got vision pro the main no the only use case i found for it really is using it in conjunction with the 14 in macbook pro because if you re not aware you can put this on your head and then connect it to your mac and then take your mac s display and put it into this massive virtual environment which means i get around this display being a little bit too small the only problem with that is that this is very heavy and uncomfortable and 32,000 quid when it comes to the uk next month so in terms of a workaround it s a it s a little bit elaborate the second thing i miss let me just put this away the second thing i miss is the battery life which i ll come on to in a moment before i get to the negatives about this laptop let s talk about the things that i love the first thing is the size and i won t keep going on about this the size of the 14 in macbook pro is perfect then the display which is in a class of its own no it s not oled and yes it has a little cutout at the top which some people seem to get immeasurably angry about trust me if you re doing work on this thing if you re doing stuff you never notice that little notch this is a beautiful display promotion is a bit of a non event to be completely honest you only spot it occasionally and and sometimes if i go back to my macbook air i then spot that things aren t quite as smooth that isn t the reason to buy this it s just a brilliant display overall connectivity is still a massive highlight of this macbook pro so we have macafe yes three thunderbolt four ports headphone jack obviously an sd card slot which i use all the time it s also got hdmi which i never ever use but it s nice to know it s there and it s all this stuff that separates this from the macbook air as much as i love that macbook air this always feels an absolute productivity beast the other thing i ve really enjoyed over the last 7 months is the black finish this is the space black version and the first thing people always ask with this thing is what are the smudges what are does it pick up fingerprints really easily and yes it does pick up fingerprints because it s black they ve got this special coating on it which is much better than whatever coating they used on the m2 matte book air this is the midnight i think it was called macbook air which gets so fingerprint so quickly i can definitely confirm that although this still picks up fingerprints they are so easy to wipe off and they don t seem to be as noticeable as they are on that macbook air but it hasn t been plain sailing with this machine which brings me on to the negative parts of it and the reason that i haven t spoken about performance yet okay so this has a m3 max chip in it which is the reason it was so expensive it s got the 16 core cpu and the 40 core gpu i also chucked in 64 gig of unified memory and that 2 tb ssd and i did the same thing with the m1 max 16 in macbook pro that was completely specked up apart from the memory and the storage you re probably wondering is there any noticeable difference between this and that 16 in macbook pro no there really isn t i did a test a little while ago where i did a render and an export test from final cut pro i can t even remember what the results are but they don t really matter it was seconds between the two machines what matters is that everyday use does it feel any quicker and the honest answer is not no not at all if anything at times it feels a bit more sluggish this is a very specific issue i found with final cut pro that is the the software that i use to edit these videos two things firstly the fans spin up much quicker on this than than they ever did on the 16 in macbook pro that is to be expected this is less thermally efficient because it s smaller that s fine the second thing is a bit more worrying and it s that i ve had these weird hangs and things happening in final cut pro where it just isn t quite as it doesn t feel quite as robust as it used to and yes this could be a software thing there might have been a software update in between me switching from the big one to the little one possibly who knows i m not sure what it is and it s not to the point where it s annoying or it s holding me back at all i ve just noticed that i seem to get a few more of those dreaded beach ball hangs when i m editing fairly big timelines or when i m exporting stuff then we have the battery life which isn t as good as the 16 in macbook pro the battery life on that 16 in macbook pro certainly the m1 max version that s the only one i ve had experience of is unbelievable a caveat here this is still wonderful when it comes to battery life if you ve come from a windows laptop or an older intel mac you will be absolutely bowled over by the battery life i m just talking as someone who s come from the 16in to the 14in this is noticeably not as good the last thing that i don t about this laptop is the price i ve never spent that much money on a computer before and yes it s completely my choice that is n apple s fault it s just it s what i decided to do but that s what happens with the macbook pro you you pick the one that you think you want to go for and then you think i need more memory or oh storage i know i can add external storage but don t don t want to carry around all those ssds no i ll put some more storage on oh it gets very very expensive and if you take into account the fact that the macbook air is brilliantly capable very few people need this much power and then we have an ipad pro which has an m4 chip which technically and yes i know you can t spec that thing up as much as you can one of these but technically the new ipad pro has a chip that is more capable newer and better than the one in this and that begs two questions one will i stick with the 14 in macbook pro and two will i stick with a macbook pro at all let s answer both of those questions very quickly the first one will i stick with the 14 in macbook pro yes and i ve had my fill of spending an absolute fortune on laptops the second question will i stick with the macbook pro this is going to be a 3 to 4 year machine for me that sounds a long time doesn t it but then you start thinking okay what happens after that will i be in a place then in let s say 3 years time where i can just start using a macbook air and then you think will we be in a position in 3 years time where i can just use the ipad pro i ve always been a macbook pro guy and i don t want these laptops to get too expensive or too niche that i can t justify owning one so in summary in 7 months of using this laptop one the power is it s enough for me i just have those slight niggles in final cut pro which most people probably weren t experience so it s not a big deal two space black doesn t smudge too much three i could have bought a much cheaper cheaper a much lower cost spec four this size is perfect for me and lastly if you ve been thinking about buying one of these for the last few weeks the last few months possibly just get it however if you re tempted to spend less money and get the m3 macbook air keep watching for my full review
value: battery and power: ['Up to 24 hours video streaming', 'Up to 16 hours wireless web', 'Built-in 72.4‑watt‑hour lithium‑polymer battery', '70W USB-C Power Adapter', '35W Dual USB-C Port Compact Power Adapter (included with M4 with 10‑core GPU; configurable with M4 with 8‑core GPU)', 'USB-C to MagSafe 3 Cable', 'Fast-charge capable with 96W or higher USB-C Power Adapter']
